items.title: NetScaler: diagnose a down VIP / service

steps.action: Confirm the VIP state
steps.expected: State DOWN / OUT OF SERVICE tells you whether it's the vserver or the bound services.

steps.action: Inspect bound services and their monitors
steps.expected: Any service UP? Any monitor failing with a clear 'last response'?

steps.action: Check connectivity from the SNIP that owns the service
steps.expected: Successful TCP handshake against the backend port.

steps.action: Look at the monitor reason
steps.expected: Specific reason (Probe timeout, HTTP 500, SSL handshake failure).

steps.action: Tail ns.log for fresh events
steps.expected: Correlate VIP/service state transitions with ns.log timestamps.

steps.action: If SSL: check cert chain and cipher negotiation
steps.expected: Full chain present, negotiated cipher matches profile.

items.title: Palo Alto: traffic is allowed but session isn't establishing

steps.action: Confirm policy match with a test
steps.expected: Security rule, NAT rule, action = allow.
commands: test security-policy-match source <src> destination <dst> protocol 6 destination-port <port>
test nat-policy-match source <src> destination <dst> protocol 6 destination-port <port>

steps.action: Verify route to destination & egress interface
steps.expected: FIB route exists and is active.
commands: test routing fib-lookup virtual-router default ip <dst>

steps.action: Find the live session
steps.expected: Session present with correct ingress/egress zones and NAT.
commands: show session all filter source <src> destination <dst>

steps.action: If no session, run a packet capture on ingress
steps.expected: SYN arrives, SYN/ACK leaves. Missing SYN/ACK → backend or NAT issue; missing SYN → upstream routing.
commands: debug dataplane packet-diag set filter match source <src> destination <dst>
debug dataplane packet-diag set filter on
debug dataplane packet-diag set capture stage receive file in.pcap
debug dataplane packet-diag set capture on

steps.action: Check for drops on the ingress interface
steps.expected: Drop counter stays flat across the test.
commands: show counter global filter packet-filter yes

steps.action: If threat prevention is suspected, look at threat logs for the session
steps.expected: No drop-IPS, drop-URL, drop-DNS entry for the flow.
commands: show log threat direction equal backward

items.title: Cisco ASA: client-side VPN client can connect but no traffic passes

steps.action: Confirm tunnel is UP on the correct group-policy
steps.expected: SA count > 0, the right tunnel-group is in use.

steps.action: Confirm the split-tunnel / tunnel-all ACL matches the resources the user needs
steps.expected: ACL includes the destination networks.

steps.action: Verify there's no NAT shadowing the VPN pool
steps.expected: NAT rule for (inside→outside) should exempt VPN pool before global NAT.

steps.action: Check that the inside hosts route back to the VPN pool via the ASA
steps.expected: Inside host shows route for VPN pool pointing to ASA inside IP.

steps.action: Packet-trace the flow both directions
steps.expected: Allow at every phase, NAT-exempt hit, no 'drop-reason'.

items.title: Cisco IOS / IOS-XE: switchport won't come up or is errdisabled

steps.action: Check interface status and reason
steps.expected: administratively down → shutdown; err-disabled → look at reason.

steps.action: If err-disabled, identify the trigger
steps.expected: bpduguard, port-security, link-flap, storm-control, udld etc.

steps.action: Fix the underlying cause (e.g. hub downstream, port-security MAC, wrong speed/duplex)
steps.expected: Safe to re-enable.

steps.action: Recover the port
steps.expected: Interface transitions to up/up.

steps.action: Optional: enable automatic recovery for this cause
steps.expected: Future occurrences auto-recover after the configured timer.

items.title: Linux: high load but nothing obvious is running

steps.action: Separate runnable vs. uninterruptible-sleep load
steps.expected: High D-state count → I/O. High R-state → CPU.

steps.action: Inspect CPU top consumers
steps.expected: Clear culprit, or very even spread across many processes.

steps.action: Look at per-CPU softirq / iowait
steps.expected: Spikes on softirq point to network; iowait points to storage.

steps.action: If iowait: which disk / which process
steps.expected: Identify hot device and PID doing the I/O.

steps.action: If softirq/network: which NIC / which queue
steps.expected: Offending CPU/queue visible.

steps.action: Check kernel log for oom / blocked-task / hung-task events
steps.expected: Any hung-task for >120s is a strong signal.

items.title: Windows: investigate a sudden network-connectivity loss on a server

steps.action: Confirm the NIC state and IP configuration
steps.expected: NIC is Up, has the expected IPv4, gateway, DNS.

steps.action: Check the default-gateway is reachable and ARP resolves
steps.expected: Ping succeeds, MAC is present.

steps.action: Verify DNS resolution path end-to-end
steps.expected: A records resolve within SLA; no SERVFAIL.

steps.action: Look at the System event log for NIC / DHCP / WLAN issues
steps.expected: Events 27/32 for NetBT, 1003/1006 for DHCP, 8003 for Browser.

steps.action: If recent change: firewall rules, route table, binding order
steps.expected: No unexpected deny rule, routes to the destination are sane.

items.title: VMware ESXi: VM network loss on a specific vSwitch / portgroup

steps.action: Confirm the VM's vNIC is connected to the right portgroup and is up
steps.expected: vNIC state is 'Connected', portgroup name matches.

steps.action: Check the vSwitch uplink state and CDP/LLDP neighbour
steps.expected: At least one uplink is Up; CDP/LLDP shows the expected upstream switchport.

steps.action: Look at portgroup VLAN and upstream trunking
steps.expected: Portgroup VLAN ID matches upstream allowed VLANs.
commands: esxcli network vswitch standard portgroup list

steps.action: Pktcap on the VM's vNIC to see inbound/outbound
steps.expected: You can see the VM's packets on the expected side; otherwise it's a vSwitch/uplink issue.

steps.action: Check vmkernel.log for portgroup / uplink / NIC teaming events
steps.expected: No 'link down', no 'port blocked' for the VM's portID.

items.title: Wireshark: isolate the 'slow web page' flow

steps.action: Capture with a tight filter to keep size manageable
steps.expected: Capture file is small enough to load quickly.

steps.action: Open in Wireshark and set a display filter for the TCP conversation
steps.expected: One conversation selected.

steps.action: Use Statistics → Conversations → TCP to find the biggest/slowest stream
steps.expected: You know which stream(s) dominate the page load.

steps.action: Review Expert Info for retransmissions, zero-window, dup-ACKs
steps.expected: Visible correlation with the slow-down.

steps.action: For TLS, check handshake time and server certificate round-trips
steps.expected: Handshake < 300 ms on a healthy path.

steps.action: For HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2, measure request-to-first-byte
steps.expected: Identify whether delay is in the request, the server response, or the body transfer.

items.title: OpenSSL: verify a certificate chain against a server

steps.action: Retrieve the chain the server actually presents
steps.expected: Each certificate printed with subject / issuer.

steps.action: Validate the full chain against the system trust store
steps.expected: Verify return code: 0 (ok).

steps.action: Inspect the leaf certificate's SAN and expiry
steps.expected: FQDN in SAN; notAfter > today + renewal window.

steps.action: Check which CA signed the leaf and whether it's trusted locally
steps.expected: Issuer appears in /etc/ssl/certs or the platform's trust bundle.

steps.action: If chain is incomplete, identify which intermediate is missing
steps.expected: AIA URL in leaf points to the intermediate that should have been sent.
commands: openssl x509 -in leaf.pem -noout -text | grep -A1 'Authority Information Access'

items.title: AWS CLI: an EC2 instance 'can't reach the internet'

steps.action: Check the instance has a public/EIP or NAT path
steps.expected: Public subnet + public IP/EIP, OR private subnet + NAT gateway in route table.

steps.action: Validate security group egress and NACL egress
steps.expected: 0.0.0.0/0 on required ports egress; NACL not blocking return.

steps.action: Confirm IGW / NAT GW is healthy
steps.expected: IGW attached to VPC; NATGW state=available.

steps.action: Run VPC Reachability Analyzer from the instance to 8.8.8.8:443
steps.expected: Path is 'reachable' end-to-end.

steps.action: From inside the instance, verify OS-level networking isn't the culprit
steps.expected: Default route present, DNS resolves.
